data.title: U.S. Population Growth Slows to Lowest Rate Since Pandemic, Census Bureau Reports
data.description:
The United States is experiencing its slowest population growth since the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic, primarily due to a decline in immigration. According to Jin10, the U.S. Census Bureau released a report on Thursday indicating that from July 2024 to July 2025, the population increased by 1.8 million, reaching a total of 342 million. This marks the smallest increase since the peak of the pandemic.

The report highlights that the majority of U.S. counties have seen a slowdown in population growth, with 40% of counties experiencing a net decrease in population during this period. The decline in immigration is cited as a significant factor impacting various economic sectors.

The findings underscore the ongoing demographic challenges facing the United States, as reduced immigration levels continue to affect population dynamics and economic growth.

data.title: US Lawmakers Propose PREDICT Act to Restrict Officials from Prediction Markets
data.description: US lawmakers have introduced the PREDICT Act, a bipartisan initiative aimed at prohibiting top US officials from participating in prediction markets. According to NS3.AI, the proposed legislation seeks to impose a 10% fine on the contract value for violations. The bill also extends its restrictions to include the spouses and dependents of these officials. This move comes amid increasing scrutiny over contracts related to politics, war, and sports.

data.title: U.S. President Trump to Miss Conservative Political Action Conference for First Time in a Decade
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump will not attend the annual Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) for the first time in ten years, according to reports from the United States on March 25. The conference is being held from March 25 to 28 in Grapevine, Texas. According to Jin10, a White House official confirmed Trump's absence from this year's event. Vice President Vance is also not listed among the speakers at the conference.

The CPAC meeting comes at a time when the Republican Party is experiencing internal divisions over U.S. military actions against Iran. Additionally, the party is preparing for a challenging midterm election year as it seeks to maintain control of both houses of Congress. CPAC is one of the most significant annual gatherings for conservative activists, officials, and media figures in the United States. Since 2016, Trump has been a regular attendee at the conference.

data.title: Blue Owl CEO Addresses Concerns Over Private Debt Defaults
data.description: Blue Owl's co-chief executive officer, Doug Ostrover, stated that the company has not experienced an increase in defaults within its loan portfolio. Bloomberg posted on X that Ostrover emphasized the importance of exercising caution amidst growing apprehensions about private debt. He reassured stakeholders that Blue Owl's loan book remains stable despite the broader market concerns. Ostrover's comments come at a time when private debt markets are under scrutiny, with analysts closely monitoring potential risks and default rates. Blue Owl continues to maintain a vigilant approach to managing its investments and assessing market conditions.

data.title: Tron DAO Expands AI Fund to $1 Billion for Infrastructure Investment
data.description:
Tron DAO has announced an expansion of its artificial intelligence fund from $100 million to $1 billion, focusing on investments in early-stage startups developing core infrastructure for the intelligent economy. According to PANews, the fund will concentrate on four key areas: agent identity systems, stablecoin-based payment channels, tokenization of real-world assets, and developer tools for autonomous financial systems.

Tron founder Justin Sun highlighted that Tron is capable of handling large-scale real-world transactions while providing a predictable, low-cost execution environment. This makes it well-suited for high-frequency, automated interaction scenarios such as fund management, merchant settlement, API-to-API payments, and other types of machine-to-machine financial flows.

data.title: U.S. Recession Risk Rises Amid Prolonged Middle East Conflict
data.description:
The ongoing conflict between the U.S., Israel, and Iran is increasing the risk of a recession in the United States, according to recent reports. According to Jin10, several institutions have raised their predictions for the likelihood of a U.S. economic downturn. Moody's Analytics now estimates a 48.6% chance of recession within the next 12 months, while Goldman Sachs has increased its forecast to 30%. Wilmington Trust and Ernst & Young Global Limited predict recession probabilities of 45% and 40%, respectively. Typically, this probability hovers around 20%.

Mark Zandi, Chief Economist at Moody's Analytics, expressed concern over the rising risk, stating that the threat of recession is becoming increasingly real. He warned that if current high oil prices persist through late May to the end of the second quarter, the U.S. economy could indeed fall into a recession.

data.title: Bhutan's Bitcoin Holdings Decline as Outflows Continue
data.description: Bhutan has transferred 519.707 BTC, valued at $36.75 million, on Wednesday, marking a continuation of its bitcoin outflows in 2026. According to NS3.AI, this recent movement brings Bhutan's total bitcoin outflows for the year to approximately $152 million. The country's bitcoin holdings have now decreased to 4,453 BTC, worth about $315 million, representing a 66% drop from a peak of roughly 13,000 BTC in late 2024.

data.title: Ukraine Faces Challenges in Securing NATO Support for U.S. Weapons Purchases
data.description: Ukraine is encountering difficulties in obtaining new commitments from NATO allies to purchase U.S. weapons, Bloomberg posted on X. The war-torn nation is seeking additional military support amid ongoing conflict, but securing these commitments has proven challenging. Despite efforts to bolster its defense capabilities, Ukraine's attempts to persuade NATO members to invest in U.S. weaponry have not yet yielded the desired results. The situation underscores the complexities of international military cooperation and the strategic considerations involved in such decisions.

data.title: Novo Chairman Lars Rebien Sorensen to Address Investors Following Boardroom Changes
data.description: Novo's Chairman Lars Rebien Sorensen is set to meet with investors at the company's annual meeting on Thursday. Bloomberg posted on X that this will be Sorensen's first appearance before investors since the recent boardroom upheaval. The meeting is anticipated to address the changes and future direction of the company. Investors are keen to hear Sorensen's insights and plans following the leadership transition.

data.title: Oil Market Volatility Linked to U.S. Policy on Iran, Analysts Say
data.description:
Investors are closely monitoring potential shifts in U.S. President Donald Trump's policy on Iran, as his social media activity continues to cause significant fluctuations in the oil market. According to Jin10, since the onset of the Middle East conflict initiated by Trump, he has frequently intensified threats against Iran during the oil market's weekend closure, while hinting at peace when oil prices rise. This pattern underscores the pivotal role of the oil market in the conflict's progression and highlights the White House's efforts to prevent runaway crude prices—successfully, at least for now.

"It's clear that Trump fears high oil prices... Gasoline prices exceeding $4 are politically fatal," said Jorge Montepeque, an oil analyst at Onyx Capital Group. "On the other hand, his ego is also important. He can't afford to appear as if he's losing."

A veteran energy trader noted a discernible pattern: whenever U.S. oil prices approach $95 to $100 per barrel, the government's conciliatory rhetoric increases, and speculation about potential government intervention in the oil market grows.

data.title: Nvidia faces class-action lawsuit for concealing cryptocurrency mining revenue.
data.description: A U.S. federal judge has granted certification to an investor class-action lawsuit against Nvidia and its CEO, Jensen Huang. The case alleges that Nvidia concealed the extent to which its gaming GPU revenue was dependent on cryptocurrency mining sales between 2017 and 2018, amounting to over $1 billion. Judge Haywood S. Gilliam Jr. ruled that Nvidia failed to demonstrate that its statements regarding cryptocurrency mining revenue had no impact on its stock price. The court cited an internal email from an Nvidia vice president who believed the company's stock price remained high due to previous statements. In 2022, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) fined Nvidia $5.5 million for failing to disclose the impact of cryptocurrency mining on its business. The plaintiffs allege that on November 15, 2018, Nvidia CFO Colette Kress acknowledged that the gaming business was underperforming and that cryptocurrency inventory clearance was taking longer than expected, causing Nvidia's stock price to fall by approximately 28.5% in the following two trading days. The case was previously dismissed in 2021, but was reinstated on appeal. Nvidia's application for review to the Supreme Court was unsuccessful. The class action lawsuit covers investors who purchased Nvidia stock between August 10, 2017 and November 15, 2018, and a case management meeting is scheduled for April 21.

data.title: Primus Labs Develops Privacy-Focused Identity Layer on BNB Chain
data.description: Primus Labs has announced its initiative to create a privacy-preserving shared identity layer on the BNB Chain. According to NS3.AI, this project is designed to introduce a real-world-based credit system to the decentralized finance sector. The development aims to enhance privacy and security while facilitating more reliable credit assessments within the DeFi ecosystem.

data.title: Japan's Central Bank to Release Monthly Core CPI Indicator
data.description: The Bank of Japan has announced plans to publish the core Consumer Price Index (CPI) on a monthly basis. According to Jin10, this move aims to provide more frequent updates on inflation trends, allowing for better analysis and decision-making. The core CPI excludes volatile items such as food and energy, offering a clearer view of underlying inflation pressures. This initiative is part of the central bank's efforts to enhance transparency and improve economic assessments.

data.title: Wheat Prices Surge Amid Heat Stress in Central U.S.
data.description: Hard-red winter wheat has reached its highest premium over the soft red variety in over seven months. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the rising temperatures in the central United States are expected to intensify the pressure on agricultural fields. This development comes as farmers and traders closely monitor weather patterns that could impact crop yields and market dynamics. The heat wave is anticipated to further strain the already stressed fields, potentially affecting the supply and pricing of wheat in the region.

data.title: Iran Conflict Influences Europe's Far-Right Electoral Strategies
data.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran is potentially altering the electoral strategies of far-right parties across Europe. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how the geopolitical tensions are reshaping political calculations. As the situation unfolds, far-right leaders are reassessing their positions and rhetoric to align with the evolving international landscape. This shift could have significant implications for upcoming elections, as parties seek to capitalize on the changing dynamics to bolster their support. The impact of the conflict is prompting a reevaluation of foreign policy stances and domestic agendas among these political groups.

data.title: EY Sets Record £188 Million Provision for Fines and Legal Claims, Financial Times Reports
data.description: EY has allocated a record £188 million to cover potential fines and legal claims, according to the Financial Times. This substantial provision highlights the firm's ongoing challenges in managing legal and regulatory issues. The move comes as EY faces scrutiny over its auditing practices and compliance with financial regulations. The provision is intended to address various legal disputes and potential penalties that may arise from these issues. EY's decision to set aside such a significant amount underscores the seriousness of the situation and the firm's commitment to resolving these matters efficiently. The Financial Times notes that this is the largest provision of its kind made by EY, reflecting the growing complexity and risk associated with the firm's operations in the current regulatory environment.

data.title: XRP Trades in Tight Range as Volatility Hits Lowest Level Since January
data.description: XRP is currently trading around $1.40, marking one of its narrowest ranges in recent months. According to NS3.AI, the cryptocurrency's volatility has reached its lowest point since January. The price has struggled to maintain levels above $1.43 and is consistently testing support near $1.40–$1.405. This situation has traders closely monitoring the potential for either a breakout or a breakdown in the near future.

data.title: Payy Secures $6 Million for Privacy-Focused Ethereum Layer 2 Solution
data.description: Payy has successfully raised $6 million to advance its privacy-focused Ethereum Layer 2 solution. According to NS3.AI, the funding round was led by FirstMark Capital, with participation from DBA Crypto. This investment aims to enhance the scalability and privacy features of Ethereum's Layer 2, addressing growing demands for secure and efficient blockchain solutions.

data.title: Airfares Surge on Major International Routes Amid Travel Disruptions
data.description: Airfares are rising sharply on key international routes, posing challenges for summer travel plans. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that disruptions and increased fuel costs are contributing to the elevated ticket prices. Travelers are facing higher expenses as airlines grapple with operational challenges and fluctuating fuel prices. The situation is affecting travel plans for many, as the cost of flying continues to climb. Industry experts suggest that these factors may persist, impacting travel budgets and decisions throughout the season.
